finding.summary: True peak exceeds 0 dBFS and spectrum shows excess low-end energy with deficient presence, indicating clipping risk and poor tonal balance.
finding.probable_cause: Raw vocal track has no processing; source audio has intersample peaks above 0 dBFS and a spectral tilt favoring low frequencies (100 Hz at –18 dBFS vs 4 kHz at –30 dBFS), likely due to microphone proximity effect or lack of high-frequency content.
finding.confidence_reason: All measured metrics are from a verified isolated capture with low silence fraction; true_peak_db (0.8 dB) is unambiguous evidence of clipping, and the spectrum shows a 12 dB drop from 100 Hz to 4 kHz, well outside typical vocal balance.

evidence_refs.description: True peak level of 0.8 dBFS exceeds 0 dBFS, indicating intersample clipping.
Third-octave levels: 100 Hz at –18 dBFS, 1 kHz at –24 dBFS, 4 kHz at –30 dBFS; low end is 12 dB hotter than high end.

proposal.reason: Reducing track volume by 1.5 dB will bring the true peak below 0 dBFS, eliminating intersample clipping. This is a safe, immediate fix while acknowledging that tonal balance still requires EQ processing (not possible via current FX chain).
